free_text: Excitation functions of 3He-particle induced reactions
on 101Ru and 102Ru for production of the medically
interesting radionuclide 101mRh
The foils of the 2-5 mg/cm**2 thickness and 16-mm
diameter made from the enriched ruthenium isotopes
were used. The enrichment of 101Ru amounted to 97.6%
and of 102Ru to 99.0%. Titanium and aluminium foils
were interspersed between the ruthenium foils: Ti for
monitoring He-3 beam and Al for the beam energy
absorption along the stack. Three stacks of 101Ru and
three of 102Ru were used.
Maximum incident energy was 34 MeV.
.The integral thick target yield of the product
radionuclide Rh-101m and long-lived impurities Rh-101g,
Rh-102m and Rh-102g were calculated from the measured
excitation functions.
HP(Ge)-detector was used to measure the product
activities in Ru samples and Ti monitor foils. The
efficiency calibration was done using the standard
radioactive sources 57Co, 133Ba, 137Cs, 152Eu, 226Ra
and 241Am.
Corrections for the number of residual nuclei leaving
the thin ruthenium foils and cross contaminations of
reactions.
The compact cyclotron CV28.
The activities of all samples were
periodically measured over a period of 2 months,
watching the half-lives and choosing the best cooling
time and the minimum dead time.
The overall uncertainty in each cross section
was calculated as a root-mean square value of the major
contributing errors associated with the measurements of
the surface density of target nuclei , incident
particle beam current, detector efficiency, counting
statistics, and decay data.
Data were received by e-mail from Dr.Ye.Skakun
UKRNDC
